## Motivation: Exploring, then Answering {.nav-open}

**Research Question**: Can enabling a person to *explore their options* before providing preferences reduce *inconsistency* in their feedback?

<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em; margin-bottom: -1em;">
<div style="flex: 1.8;">
![](images/morl-bruce.png){width="100%"}
<div style="margin-top: -1em;">
[@moplayground]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}
</div>
</div>
<div style="flex: 1.2;">
[Constructed preferences]{style="text-decoration: underline;"}. People's preferences may not formalize on first glance--especially for non-experts.

[@constructed]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}
</div>
</div>

<div style="text-align: center;">
**Idea**: develop an algorithm with a dedicated exploration phase to allow people to construct their preferences.
</div>
